template_data.headline_html: Five things small businesses get wrong about set-asides.

items.title: 8(a) ends at year nine
items.sub: Your graduation date is set on approval day — plan your revenue around that exit.

items.title: HUBZone requires a live address
items.sub: Principal office and 35% of employees must actually reside in a HUBZone area.

items.title: SDVOSB is two separate processes
items.sub: VA verification and SBA self-certification are distinct — missing one leaves you exposed.

items.title: WOSB narrows competition, not evaluators
items.sub: Capability statement, past performance, and pricing still decide the award.

items.title: Certification stacking is legal
items.sub: WOSB + HUBZone + 8(a) can qualify you for four set-aside types. Most businesses never check.

template_data.image_headline: CERTIFICATIONS OPEN DOORS. YOUR STRATEGY WINS THEM.
template_data.image_prompt: Infographic checklist layout. Dark navy background. Bold headline top: 'CERTIFICATIONS OPEN DOORS. STRATEGY WINS THEM.' with key icon. Left side: illustrated professional woman in blazer holding a certificate document and reviewing a clipboard. Right side: 5 checklist rows with green checkmarks and short labels: '8(A) TIMELINE', 'HUBZONE ADDRESS', 'SDVOSB TWO STEPS', 'WOSB FIELD NARROWED', 'STACK THEM'. Bottom dark band with callout: 'CERT IS THE DOOR.' Footer text: 'Josh Ladick · GSA Focus'.
